{{Quote|You ''have'' to know, Tu'ala…why you would sacrifice ''yourself'' for another… It ''isn't'' that the Force loves you ''less'' than other creatures… It is that ''you'' love other creatures more.|Zuth|<ref name="TL" />}}
'''Zuth''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the final years of both institutions.

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Zuth was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Completing years of training, crafting for himself a yellow-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], and passing the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]], Zuth was elevated to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. Serving the Order valiantly as a Knight, Zuth selected [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiate]] [[Tu'ala]] as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and began to tutor her in the Jedi ways as they traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] together.<ref name="TL" />

As Tu'ala's mentor, Zuth attempted to instill in his student the notion that the death of a single individuals might be necessary to ensure that others fulfilled their destines. Tu'ala did not take quickly to this lesson, though Zuth was patient with her. His time as her teacher fell short as he fell in battle defending his Padawan's life during an assassination attempt on an unspecified senator; dying so that she may live on. His body was returned to the Temple where it was [[Jedi funeral/Legends|cremated]] in the Jedi custom.<ref name="TL" />

==Legacy==
Not long after his death, the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] declared war on each other. In the opening battle, the masterless Tu'ala finally embraced her deceased master's teachings and sacrificed her life in the [[Petranaki arena]] so that fellow Padawan [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] might live. Her action went unnoticed during the confusion of battle, but her deed would go onto shape the flow of galactic history, including the completion of the Prophecy of the [[Chosen One/Legends|Chosen One]].<ref name="TL" />

==Appearances==
